title: tetramolopium lepidotum subsp. lepidotum
intro: Tetramolopium lepidotum subsp. lepidotum, also known as Hawaiian lehua fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Hawaiian and Polynesian cultures, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Tetramolopium lepidotum subsp. lepidotum include treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and pneumonia. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as an infusion or a poultice, and in some cases, it is used as a topical application or added to baths for its medicinal and therapeutic benefits.
